chapter_voice_summary: Across the 13 chapter-2 verses available (2.1-2.10, 2.46, 2.47, 2.48), the voice is consistent: didactic, corrective, slightly stern, and structural rather than consoling. BG 2.48 maintains this voice through specific corrective moves: 'The work is not optional. The state is not optional. Either alone is the failure mode' is a senior-teacher refusal of both popular misreadings (passive-quietism and grasping-effort). The falsifiability clause ('An on-call engineer who reads no signal off the page beyond red metric, run runbook has confused stoic suppression with samatvam') is direct and named-failure-mode rather than gentle. The 'dashboard moves; the operator does not' quotable line is structural-architectural, not aphoristic — it is the kind of compressed senior-engineer formulation the chapter calls for. Compared to 2.47 (which has 'Your right is to the deploy, not to the launch metrics') the voice is identical: terse, structural, falsifiable. No tonal break. Pass.
